Title: RoS orders provisional dissolution of MTUC
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Registrar of Societies (RoS) has ordered the Malaysian Trades Union Congress' (MTUC) registration to be provisionally dissolved after it allegedly failed to comply with a directive to submit key documents.
According to an order dated today, sighted by the New Straits Times, RoS said MTUC failed to furnish two copies of its audited financial records and balance sheets from 2020 to 2025.
MTUC also failed to submit two copies of a detailed report declaring any foreign funding or assets received between 2020 and 2025, including money, property or benefits provided by individuals residing abroad, foreign organisations, or foreign governments and their agencies.
"As such, the registration of the organisation is hereby ordered to be provisionally dissolved with effect from the date of this order," the notice stated.
The provisional dissolution followed a directive issued on April 6 requiring MTUC to submit a wide range of information within 30 days, including membership records from 2020 to 2025, meeting minutes and details of external affiliations.
The directive also required MTUC to provide full records of office bearers, as well as minutes from its Triennial Delegates Conference and Special Delegates Conference.
The order was issued under Section 14(5) of the Societies Act 1966, which empowers RoS to take action against societies that fail to provide required particulars, returns or documents.
MTUC is the country's largest umbrella body representing trade unions and workers' interests.
The New Straits Times has reached out to MTUC for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysian completes 50,000km solo global journey in Perodua Kenari
Article:
KUANTAN: A digital media designer has etched his name into the country's history after completing a solo journey of some 46,000km across 32 countries over 736 days.
Jack Voon Kuet Fui achieved the feat behind the wheel of a 20-year-old Perodua Kenari.
He began his journey from the state capital on May 1, 2024, and completed his globetrotting expedition today.
The 49-year-old earned a place in the Malaysia Book of Records as the first Malaysian to circumnavigate the globe in a locally made car.
The Sarawak-born adventurer, who had been saving for five years prior to embarking on the road trip, said he spent close to RM600,000 and was grateful to return to Malaysia safely with his Kenari.
"My curiosity about countries around the world led me on this journey. I had read a lot about foreign countries but had only seen them through television, magazines and newspapers.
"This trip was an eye-opener for me as I learned and experienced many things. While travelling in Europe, I performed busking using the sape (a traditional Sarawak musical instrument) to raise funds," he said when met at Menara Kuantan 188 here today.
Voon said he also had the opportunity to promote Malaysia abroad, including spending three months in China in 2024 during the 50th anniversary of Malaysia-China diplomatic relations, where he was featured on several foreign television channels.
Meanwhile, he said he typically drove about eight hours a day, covering an average of 300km daily.
To save costs, he stayed in a rooftop tent mounted on his Kenari and cooked simple meals during overnight stops.
"After leaving Malaysia in May 2024, I drove through Thailand and continued across Asia, Africa, South America and Europe. My last stop was Chile before returning to Malaysia," he said.
Voon said he endured a series of harrowing incidents along the way, including the theft of his money and a bag containing recordings of his journey in two separate South American countries.
"While driving in Africa, I faced a dangerous encounter where individuals allegedly attempted to throw themselves onto my moving car in an apparent bid for compensation. I managed to avoid a potentially serious crash," he said, adding that overall, most people he met were friendly and helpful.
He expressed gratitude to the Pahang government, including Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail, state Unity, Tourism and Culture Committee chairman Leong Yu Man, as well as friends who supported him throughout his journey, including when his car broke down abroad.
Now that he has completed his global expedition, Voon plans to travel around Malaysia later this month. However, this time he intends to take things at a slower pace and has not set any deadline for completion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azman Hashim installed as new OUM chancellor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Banking figure Tan Sri Azman Hashim was installed as the new chancellor of Open University Malaysia (OUM) during the first session of the university's 30th Convocation Ceremony today.
Azman, who previously served as pro-chancellor of the university for about two decades, will present scrolls to some of the 7,030 graduates at the convocation.
In a statement today, OUM said the four-day convocation celebrates graduates from various backgrounds, including elderly students in their 70s as well as a Master of Business Administration (MBA) graduate from Kajang Prison.
Among the graduates who drew attention were the oldest graduate, Muhammad Tasan, 75, who received a Master of Management, and medical doctor Dr Loh Yunn Hua, 72, who obtained a Bachelor of Psychology with Honours.
Their achievements were recognised through the Lifelong Learning Award, which highlights the university's emphasis on continuous education.
The first session also honoured Siti Hadjar Mansor Awang Hitam, a female bodybuilder who completed a Bachelor of Psychology with Honours.
Meanwhile, OUM President and Vice-Chancellor Prof Datuk Dr Ahmad Izanee Awang said the diverse backgrounds of the graduates reflected the university's mission to expand access to higher education regardless of age or personal circumstances.
"From teachers, nurses and other professionals to celebrities, athletes and even prison inmates, our students are the best example of OUM's motto - University for All.
"Our open, distance and digital education model has enabled many individuals to work and study at the same time. We hope OUM will continue to be embraced by more Malaysians in the years ahead," he said in the statement.
– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KLM flight attendant tested for hantavirus
Article:
THE HAGUE: A flight attendant for the KLM airline is being tested for the hantavirus after showing mild symptoms and being admitted to hospital in Amsterdam, a Dutch health ministry spokesperson told AFP Thursday.
The woman was undergoing tests in hospital, said the spokesperson, Mischa Stubenitsky.
RTL media reported that the flight attendant had come into contact with a Dutch woman who was taken off a KLM plane and later died of the virus in South Africa.
KLM said on Wednesday that the passenger had been briefly on a flight from Johannesburg to the Netherlands, but was removed before take-off.
"Due to the passenger's medical condition at the time, the crew decided not to allow the passenger to travel on the flight," which was flight KL592 from Johannesburg to Amsterdam on April 25 at 11.15pm local time.
"After the passenger was removed from the aircraft, the flight departed for the Netherlands," added KLM.
Dutch health authorities are contacting people on the flight "as a precaution," KLM said in its statement. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Elderly man found dead in Sungai Petani after extensive search
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: An 83-year-old man who went missing near his home in Kampung Masjid, Tikam Batu, 5 days ago was found dead today.
Tikam Batu Fire and Rescue Station chief Assistant Superintendent Fauzi Shuaib said the victim was initially believed to have fallen into a drain near his home.
The victim, Mohd Khalid @ Halim Salleh, was found dead near an abandoned house in Kampung Padang, Tikam Batu.
Fauzi said the man was believed to have wandered off and become disoriented before his death.
Search and rescue (SAR) efforts began on Sunday morning.
The victim's daughter, Rosmawati Mohd Khalid, 51, said her father suffered from heart disease and lived alone.
She expressed relief that his body was found.
His remains were sent to the forensic department at Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital in Alor Star for a post-mortem.
Police said a missing person report was lodged on May 2.
Kuala Muda acting police chief Superintendent S. Saravanan said no criminal elements were detected in the case.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MMEA detains fishing vessel over licence breach, foreign crew
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) has detained a Class C local fishing vessel suspected of breaching licence conditions and unlawfully employing foreign crew.
Penang Maritime director Captain (Maritime) Muhammad Suffi Mohd Ramli said the vessel was intercepted about 2.7 nautical miles south of Pulau Kendi during a routine patrol last night.
He said MMEA personnel stopped the vessel after detecting it operating in the area before conducting a thorough inspection.
"Checks revealed that the vessel was operated by five Myanmar nationals, including the skipper, aged between 22 and 38. One of them also failed to produce valid identification documents," he said today.
Muhammad Suffi said preliminary findings showed the vessel had breached its licence conditions by employing foreign crew without approval from the Director-General of Fisheries Malaysia.
The case is being investigated under Section 8(b) of the Fisheries Act 1985 for illegal employment of foreign crew, as well as Section 15(1) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for failing to ensure valid work permits on board.
"All crew members, along with the vessel, estimated to be worth about RM3 million, have been detained and handed over to MMEA Penang investigating officers for further action," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysians can convert foreign driving license from June 1
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Malaysians holding valid foreign driving licences with no restrictions will be allowed to apply for conversion to Malaysian Driving Licences (LMM) beginning June 1.
In a statement today, the Transport Ministry (MOT) said applications under the revised procedures can be made at all Road Transport Department (RTD) offices nationwide.
"The move is in line with current developments and aims to ensure the mobility of Malaysians working, residing or managing affairs abroad is not disrupted," the ministry said.
Under the current policy, conversion of foreign driving licences is limited to Malaysians who obtained foreign licences before holding Malaysian licences, Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) participants and members of the diplomatic corps.
MOT said the improved procedures are intended to ensure compliance with existing regulations while maintaining driving competency standards among applicants.
"It is also aimed at facilitating applications for the conversion of foreign driving licences to Malaysian licences by taking into account current needs and the mobility of Malaysians, especially amid the current challenging global and domestic economic environment," it said.
The ministry said application requirements, procedures and fees will be available on the JPJ website from June 1.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: In OpenAI trial, Altman accused of sowing 'chaos,' distrust among executives
Article:
SAN FRANCISCO: A former OpenAI technology chief testified on Wednesday in Elon Musk's lawsuit that CEO Sam Altman sowed distrust among top executives as the company forged ahead with developing and broadly deploying its powerful artificial intelligence software.
"My concern was about Sam saying one thing to one person and completely the opposite to another person," said Mira Murati, who was briefly CEO of OpenAI after its board temporarily forced out Altman in 2023.
She said Altman was "creating chaos" and, at times, was deceptive with her and others.
Murati's recorded testimony was played in Oakland, California, federal court, in the second week of a trial.
Musk, the world's richest person, sued OpenAI in 2024 on the grounds that it improperly became a for-profit company, abandoned charitable goals and should turn back into a nonprofit.
If successful, Musk could benefit by hindering the commercial ambitions of a competitor to his own startup xAI, which is now part of SpaceX
The trial could determine the future of OpenAI, which, as a leading AI firm, has tremendous influence over development of the advanced software and its deployment into schools, government agencies and businesses.
Musk, a co-founder of OpenAI, is seeking US$150 billion in damages to be paid by OpenAI and investor Microsoft to benefit the startup's charitable arm.
Murati, who left OpenAI and has co-founded her own AI startup, said Altman pitted executives against one another and undermined her role as technology chief.
But she said she wanted him to remain CEO, and pressed board members for a fuller justification for ousting him in 2023.
"OpenAI was at catastrophic risk of falling apart," Murati said. "I was concerned about the company completely blowing up."
Another former OpenAI official, onetime board member Shivon Zilis, hinted at some of the turmoil as the company prepared for the blockbuster release of chatbot ChatGPT.
She said the board "voiced extreme concern" about releasing ChatGPT "without any semblance of board communication."
Asked whether she raised concerns about Altman internally, Zilis said "there had been a couple of instances."
Zilis now works for Musk's Neuralink. She is also the mother of four of his children.
Testimony from Musk, OpenAI President Greg Brockman and others has sketched out a series of conflicts among senior executives and founders about how to drive and support growth of the company and whether Musk, whose early funding was crucial to launching the startup, should become CEO.
The trial has also brought surprises to light. Musk, for instance, attempted to settle with Brockman days before the trial began, and at one point felt like "a fool" for continuing to fund OpenAI.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gaza malnutrition crisis devastates pregnant women, infants, says medical organisation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The worsening malnutrition crisis in Gaza has had a devastating impact on pregnant women, newborns and infants, said Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF).
MSF said medical data collected between 2024 and early 2026 showed alarming increases in premature births, low birth weight, miscarriages and infant deaths among mothers affected by malnutrition during pregnancy.
It said more than half of the 201 mothers studied in neonatal intensive care units at Al Nasser and Al Helou hospitals experienced malnutrition at some point during pregnancy, with a quarter still affected at the time of delivery.
Nearly 90 per cent of babies born to these mothers were premature, while 84 per cent had low birth weight. Neonatal mortality among these infants was twice as high as among babies born to mothers who were not malnourished.
MSF linked the crisis to restrictions on food, aid and medical supplies, as well as attacks on civilian infrastructure.
"The malnutrition crisis is entirely man-made. Before the war, malnutrition in Gaza was almost non-existent.
"For 2.5 years, the systematic blockade of humanitarian aid and commercial goods, coupled with insecurity, has severely restricted access to food and clean water," said MSF medical referent for emergencies Mercè Rocaspana.
Separate data from MSF-supported facilities in Khan Younis showed that between October 2024 and Dec 2025, 513 infants aged below six months were admitted for malnutrition treatment. Of these, 91 per cent were at risk of poor growth and development.
By the end of the period, 200 infants had dropped out of treatment programmes, with 32 per cent defaulting largely due to insecurity and displacement. Only 48 per cent recovered, while seven per cent died.
MSF medical coordinator for Palestine Marina Pomares said food insecurity was so widespread that many mothers sought nutritional support even before their children were diagnosed with malnutrition.
"Families adopted coping mechanisms, often prioritising men and children over mothers when distributing limited food," she said.
The humanitarian group also reported a sharp rise in malnutrition cases since early 2024, with nearly 5,000 children aged below 15 admitted for acute malnutrition treatment, alongside more than 3,400 pregnant and breastfeeding women enrolled in nutrition programmes.
One patient, identified as Mona, said her five-month-old son died from severe malnutrition, while she herself suffered from poor nutrition, illness and extreme hardship during pregnancy.
The crisis worsened after the collapse of a ceasefire in March 2025, with food distribution points drastically reduced and access to food becoming even more restricted.
MSF said its facilities later recorded a surge in patients injured while attempting to obtain food, alongside rising malnutrition cases linked to food deprivation.
The situation has also taken a psychological toll, with many women reporting extreme stress and anxiety, contributing to an increase in miscarriages.
Between October and November last year, around three-quarters of Gaza's population were estimated to be facing acute food insecurity, with famine conditions declared — the first such declaration in the Middle East.
MSF warned that despite the current ceasefire, the situation remained fragile, with new malnutrition cases continuing to emerge.
The organisation called on Israeli authorities and allied countries to allow the sustained and unhindered entry of humanitarian aid and essential supplies into Gaza.
It said that restoring access to food, healthcare and basic necessities is critical to preventing further deterioration in public health and ensuring dignity for the population.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar attends BIMP-EAGA Summit to drive regional development
Article:
CEBU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim attended the Brunei Darussalam-Indonesia-Malaysia-Philippines East Asean Growth Area (BIMP-EAGA) Summit here today as part of efforts to strengthen regional development and economic cooperation.
The summit brought together leaders from the member nations to discuss agendas regarding sustainable development, connectivity, and inclusive economic growth within the sub-region.
Anwar was accompanied by Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan and the Foreign Ministry's secretary-general, Datuk Seri Amran Mohamed Zin.
Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. said that cooperation under BIMP-EAGA must continue to be strengthened to ensure that planned developments truly benefit the people.
He said the BIMP-EAGA Vision 2035, which aligns with Asean aspirations, is not merely an economic integration plan but a commitment to ensuring development is more sustainable and felt by communities at the grassroots level.
"Key programmes and projects currently being implemented have brought significant changes to communities through job creation, strengthened connectivity, and expanded economic opportunities, especially in the sub-regional growth areas," he said during his opening speech at the summit today.
Marcos Jr. said that these successes prove regional cooperation has the greatest impact when its benefits are felt by local communities.
He also called on member nations to renew their commitment to translating vision into concrete action to ensure the BIMP-EAGA region remains interconnected, resilient, and prosperous for future generations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fresh landfill fire erupts in Sungai Pinang hours after first blaze extinguished
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A fresh fire broke out at a disused landfill along Lebuh Sungai Pinang here today, hours after firefighters extinguished a similar blaze at the same site.
Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director John Sagun Francis said firefighters received a distress call on the latest incident at 8.06am.
He said a team from the Jalan Perak fire and rescue station arrived at the scene at 8.13am and found four sectors of burning rubbish piles at the landfill near a condominium.
"The fire involved piles of rubbish, with the affected areas divided into four sectors to facilitate extinguishing works.
"Firefighters carried out direct attack operations to contain the blaze from spreading further, with assistance from an excavator belonging to the Penang Island City Council (MBPP)," he said in a statement today.
He said the blaze was brought under control at 10.02am, with all fire sectors fully extinguished by 3.16pm.
The operation ended at 4.32pm after firefighters confirmed the area was safe.
A total of nine firefighters, including supervising officers and the station chief, were involved in the operation using a Compact Fire Rescue Tender and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services vehicle.
Personnel from the Department of Environment, MBPP and the Bukit Bendera volunteer fire brigade also assisted in the operation.
No casualties were reported.
Earlier today, the department said a
fire involving solid waste at the same disused landfill
was fully extinguished after a more than seven-hour operation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: India state polls deepen Hindu-Muslim political divide, results show
Article:
INDIA'S main opposition Congress party drew increasingly strong backing from Muslim voters while Hindus overwhelmingly voted for Prime Minister Narendra Modi's party, results from recent elections in four states have shown.
This voter trend highlights hardening religious polarisation in the officially secular nation, and the deep-seated ideological division between India's two biggest political parties, analysts and political commentators say.
Modi adopted an unabashedly pro-Hindu platform to take power in 2014, and his Bharatiya Janata Party largely follows a Hindu-first ideology called Hindutva.
Such voter fragmentation helps expand its dominance across the country because Hindus are nearly 80 per cent of India's 1.42 billion people compared with about 14 per cent Muslims.
"The rise of the BJP has led to a consolidation of Muslim voters behind so-called secular parties, particularly the Congress - a form of reverse polarisation is taking place," said political analyst Rasheed Kidwai, visiting fellow with the Observer Research Foundation.
Muslim leaders and analysts say voters from that community are increasingly choosing Congress or other strong regional parties over smaller parties that focus on their interests but have struggled to be part of any governments in recent years.
Congress fared poorly in the elections held last month across four states and one federal territory, with results declared this week.
Its alliance secured control of only one state, while a BJP-led coalition won three and a new regional outfit took the remaining contest.
The Muslim support for Congress was most evident in the BJP-ruled northeastern state of Assam, where 18 of its 19 newly elected lawmakers are from the community, up from roughly 16 in the previous assembly.
The party had fielded 20 Muslim candidates and about 80 non-Muslims for the 126-member legislature, in which the BJP won 82 seats.
The Assam-based All India United Democratic Front (AIUDF), which mainly draws Muslim support, saw its tally collapse to just two seats from 16 five years earlier.
[[nid:1434408]]
In neighbouring West Bengal, which the BJP won for the first time with 207 lawmakers in a 294-member assembly, the two Congress legislators elected were Muslims.
The BJP did not field any Muslim candidates in either Assam or West Bengal. Party leaders in both states, including the prospective chief minister of Bengal, Suvendu Adhikari, credited the victories to support from Hindu voters.
"In the future - depending on local political dynamics - if Muslim voters increasingly consolidate behind Congress, dominant Hindu voters may also regroup more strongly around the BJP," said political columnist Radhika Ramaseshan.
Congress has capitalised on the fear and insecurity felt by many Muslim voters under BJP rule where they feel marginalised and their citizenship questioned, said Badruddin Ajmal, chief of the AIUDF in Assam.
"The argument being made is that only a party with the strength to fight the BJP at the centre can ultimately address these concerns. This is not true but voters believe it because they are scared."
After the BJP accused Congress of becoming a "new Muslim League", Congress said Muslims account for about 12 per cent of its 664 state legislators nationwide, compared with roughly 78 per cent who are Hindus, in line with India's religious makeup.
"I am embarrassed to talk about these things in the 21st century," said Congress spokesperson Pawan Khera, stressing that his party, which has ruled India for 54 years since independence in 1947, had broad support.
"We have always stood by the weak and the oppressed and will continue to do so, irrespective of their religion and caste."
The BJP too has often reached out to Muslim voters, although it did not field any Muslim candidate in the 2024 general election.
Ramaseshan, the columnist, said communal rhetoric, especially closer to elections, has become much more marked under Modi than in past BJP regimes.
"The BJP and the larger Sangh (the party's ideological parent) are shaping a new idea of India as a 'Hindu rashtra (nation)' — and that narrative has increasingly embedded itself in public consciousness," she said.
"In the years ahead, we may see a complete overturning of the very idea of India."
* The writers are from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Repeated floods leave PJ food court traders traumatised
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Repeated flash floods at the Selera Jaya 223 food court here have left traders anxious whenever dark clouds gather, following what they described as the worst flooding incident in years yesterday.
Checks at the food court, which houses about 30 active stalls, found that floodwaters had fully receded, with traders busy cleaning up after the deluge, which rose to chest level.
Trader Che Adin Ahmad, 74, said the area had been severely inundated four times over the past two months, including yesterday.
He said he suffered losses of more than RM2,000 each time floods hit the area, adding that the location was no longer safe for business operations.
"It floods here frequently, but the water usually remains below knee level. However, recently we have experienced severe flooding episodes and yesterday was probably the worst.
"The water rose to my neck at one point and almost everything in my stall was submerged," said Che Adin, who has been trading there for more than a decade.
He said the water rose rapidly at 3pm before receding by 4.30pm, but the damage had already been done.
He urged the local authorities to consider relocating the food court.
"It is no longer safe to do business here. I hope the local authorities will take action.
"They need to help us because many B40 patrons come here to eat," he said when met.
Dasuki Mat Jusoh, 58, claimed the worsening floods were linked to ongoing road and drainage works nearby.
He said traders had been informed by the Public Works Department that excavation works in the area had caused drains to become clogged.
"When water cannot flow properly, it overflows and enters our shops very quickly," said Dasuki, who has been trading at the food court for about 30 years.
He said he suffered losses of up to RM3,000 after stocks of mutton, chicken, beef and bones were damaged in yesterday's flood.
Goh Kim Khaing, 75, said his losses could reach RM5,000, adding that the past two months had brought the worst flooding he had witnessed since he began trading there 36 years ago.
"For 36 years, I have been doing business here and flash floods this severe have never happened before. It all started this year," he said.
Yesterday's heavy rain triggered flash
floods
across the Klang Valley, including several parts of Petaling Jaya.
At the Selera Jaya 223 food court in Section 51A, floodwaters reportedly entered several
stalls
and partially submerged vehicles in the parking area.
In response, Petasling Jaya City Council (MBPJ) said its relevant units had been deployed to carry out clean-up, monitoring and risk
mitigation
works at all affected locations in the city.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Sultan of Selangor makes it clear, no consent for pig farming in state
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah has once again reiterated that he does not consent to pig farming activities in Selangor, consistent with his royal decree made in February.
In a statement, the sultan's private secretary Datuk Mohamad Munir Bani said the ruler's position was made after taking into account the implications of pig farming, including the risk of pollution to land, air and river water sources through the discharge of manure and wastewater.
"The Sultan of Selangor maintains the stand expressed in the Feb 10 press statement that he does not consent to any pig farming activity being carried out in any district in Selangor.
"The Sultan also expressed concern that such activities could affect water catchment areas and rivers which serve as sources of clean water supply to about nine million people in Selangor, Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya.
"Moreover, modern farming methods which comply with proper standards would involve very high costs, making them impractical if they were only meant to meet pork supply needs in Selangor," he said.
He said Sultan Sharafuddin had suggested that pork be imported from other states, such as Sarawak, to meet the needs of non-Muslims in Selangor who consume pork.
"Even countries like Singapore do not carry out pig farming and import pork from abroad.
"The Sultan of Selangor is also of the view that pig farming, whether on a large or small scale, is unsuitable in Selangor given the state's limited land resources and high property value, which should be used for more economic development that benefits society as a whole," he added.
Munir said Sultan Sharafuddin had decreed that the issue of pig farming be brought to a full stop to avoid public unease and to ensure peace and harmony in Selangor is preserved.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia, Cambodia deepen trade, investment links via MCBA-FASMEC partnership
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia and Cambodia are stepping up business cooperation, expanding opportunities across Asean and boosting cross-border partnerships, trade and investment.
This follows a memorandum of understanding (MoU) between the Malaysia-Cambodia Business Association (MCBA) and the Federation of Associations for Small and Medium Enterprises of Cambodia (FASMEC).
MCBA founding president Datuk Seri Ricky Yaw said the partnership aims to create a platform delivering measurable value to businesses across Malaysia, Cambodia and the wider Asean region.
He said MCBA and FASMEC will host the Cambodia International Leadership Forum in Phnom Penh in mid-2027, positioning it as a regional platform bringing together business leaders, technology innovators, traders, investors and entrepreneurs from across Asean.
"This platform will be designed with one clear objective — not just to talk, but to translate ideas into action. This is important. Today in Asean, opportunities are not lacking," he said at the MCBA Annual General Meeting and Installation Night Dinner here today.
Meanwhile, Commercial Attaché at the Royal Embassy of Cambodia to Malaysia Vith Panhnha, representing Cambodian Ambassador to Malaysia Ouk Chandara, said Cambodia hopes MCBA will help strengthen business and trade ties this year, further expanding bilateral collaboration.
He said Cambodian small and medium enterprises (SMEs) are seeking to increase trade with Malaysia and broaden engagement through agreements with various partners.
"Beyond Asean free trade arrangements and agreements with Asean partners, Cambodia has several major trade agreements, including the Cambodia-China Free Trade Agreement, the Cambodia-Korea Free Trade Agreement and the Cambodia-United Arab Emirates Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement.
"We hope ongoing negotiations will lead to more agreements soon and further expand Cambodia's market globally," he added.
Echoing the view, Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation (Matrade) deputy chief executive officer Jai Shankar said both countries share a common goal of accelerating the growth of Malaysian companies in the two markets.
He said that while geopolitical developments could affect global trade, they may also strengthen intra-Asean ties.
"Sometimes, when neighbours are very close, they may not fully appreciate each other's potential. But today, there is a growing need for us to view one another more strategically. Anything that supports this is beneficial," he said.
He added that Cambodia offers strong opportunities, describing it as a vibrant economy that could serve as a gateway to broader global markets.
"We are looking not only at trade and investment, but also at encouraging Malaysian companies to consider Cambodia as a sourcing destination," he said.
In the first quarter of 2026, total Malaysia-Cambodia trade rose 49 per cent to RM1.68 billion (US$430 million) from RM1.13 billion (US$250 million) a year earlier.
Trade between Malaysia and Cambodia expanded, with Malaysian exports rising 46.0 per cent to RM1.30 billion and imports increasing 59.6 per cent to RM380 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Philippines warns China research ship in disputed waters
Article:
MANILA: The Philippine Coast Guard said Thursday it deployed aircraft to issue radio warnings to a Chinese research ship in a disputed area of the South China Sea "swarming" with vessels from Beijing's so-called maritime militia.
The research vessel Xiang Yang Hong 33, which is capable of supporting submersible craft, was operating near a reef in the contested Spratly Island chain, the Philippine Coast Guard said.
The Chinese ship was deploying a service boat towards the Spratlys' Iroquois Reef on Wednesday when it was spotted by a Coast Guard plane, "confirming ongoing unauthorised (marine scientific research) operations", the statement said.
Iroquois Reef is located at the southern end of Reed Bank, an area in the Spratlys thought to be rich in natural gas and oil.
During the flight, Manila's Coast Guard also spotted 41 "maritime militia vessels" anchored in waters surrounding Iroquois Reef and Philippine-occupied Thitu Island, home to about 400 Filipinos and a new coast guard station.
"The Philippines has not granted such consent to the (People's Republic of China) for any (marine scientific research) activities in these waters," the Coast Guard said.
"The presence of the research vessel and the large-scale swarming by Chinese Maritime Militia vessels represent a serious infringement on Philippine sovereign rights and maritime jurisdiction."
The Chinese Embassy in Manila did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
The Xiang Yang Hong 33, which left China over three weeks ago, has been monitored conducting surveys near various Philippine-claimed features in the Spratly chain, including the flashpoint Sabina Shoal and Second Thomas Shoal.
Beijing claims almost the entire South China Sea, despite an international ruling that its assertion has no legal basis, and defends its actions against Philippine vessels as lawful and proportional.
It has been deploying navy, coast guard and maritime militia forces – allegedly Chinese fishing vessels – in a bid to bar the Philippines from strategically important reefs and islands in the disputed waterway.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PUNB cuts Prosper Grow financing rate to ease SME burden
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Perbadanan Usahawan Nasional Bhd (PUNB) has reduced the profit rate for its Prosper Grow business financing scheme to 3.5 per cent per annum to ease cost pressures on Bumiputera entrepreneurs.
The revised rate, lowered from between five per cent and 6.75 per cent previously, will take effect for all financing approvals from Jan 1, 2026, including existing recipients approved this year.
PUNB said the initiative is expected to benefit Bumiputera entrepreneurs in 2026, particularly those requiring financing between RM100,000 and RM1 million to grow their businesses.
The reduction of between 1.5 per cent and 3.25 per cent of the initial profit rate translates into meaningful cost savings for businesses.
This enables improved cash flow management, stronger financial resilience, and greater capacity to reinvest in growth, it said.
PUNB chief executive officer Izwan Zainuddin said the adjustment reflects a much needed and timely intervention to ensure Bumiputera enterprises remain competitive in a challenging economic environment.
Izwan said reducing the rate to 3.5 per cent is more than just a financial incentive but it is a vital step toward ensuring Bumiputera entrepreneurs remain resilient, competitive and capable of long-term growth.
"PUNB remains committed to serving as a relevant and responsive facilitator for the evolving needs of the business community," he said.
The revised financing structure also marks an early implementation of PUNB's R30 Strategic Plan (2026-2030), aimed at strengthening its financing and business support ecosystem.
Under the realignment, PUNB now offers three core financing schemes namely, Prosper Grow, Prosper Great and Prosper Impact/Nova, tailored to different stages of business growth.
Entrepreneurs can obtain more information on the financing schemes and eligibility requirements through PUNB's official website.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police seek remand extension for two suspects in Ketereh student murder
Article:
KOTA BARU: Police will seek to extend the remand of two 19-year-old suspects over the murder of a college student found with 61 stab wounds in Ketereh last Friday.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the suspects would be brought before the Kota Baru magistrate's court tomorrow morning.
"We will apply for remand extension to allow further investigations to be carried out," he said here today.
On Friday, the
body of Nurfisya Zulkifly, also 19, was found in Jalan Kampung Kubang Kiat, Ketereh
, about 12.30am.
She had 61 stab wounds in various parts of her body.
Police then detained four people, including a couple. The other two were the couple's son, who was the victim's boyfriend and another youth.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: National Social Policy Action Plan must connect policies to people's lives, says Zahid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The National Social Policy Action Plan 2026–2030, which was launched today, must be a bridge between the policies that the government has framed and the lives that the people live, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
He said the plan must ensure that assistance does not overlap, information is not interrupted, vulnerable groups are not left out and every intervention truly reaches those in need.
"A strong society is not built programme by programme, but household by household. Therefore, our shift must be clear from policy to action, from programme to solutions and from assistance to empowerment.
"Because ultimately, social policy is only valuable when it can change the lives of the people," he said at the launch today.
Present were Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri, her deputy Lim Hui Ying and Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad.
He said the previously launched National Social Policy provided direction, while the National Social Policy Action Plan offers a more organised framework to give meaning to the policy's principles.
"The National Social Policy Action Plan must be a clear work plan, with 102 initiatives centred on four main focuses: prevention, empowerment, promotion and protection.
"Through it, we cannot just wait for problems to get worse. We must prevent early, empower people to rise, promote good values in society and protect the vulnerable so that no one is left out."
Zahid also called for three major shifts.
"First, from aid to empowerment, by moving beyond dependency to enabling people to work, do business and build their own futures.
"Second, from silos to an ecosystem, by expanding collaboration beyond ministries to include local authorities, non-governmental organisations, the private sector, academia and communities, since social issues require collective action.
"Third, from reactive to preventive, by acting early to prevent family breakdown, school dropouts, elderly neglect and youth problems instead of responding only after crises occur."
In his speech, Zahid said today's social challenges are interconnected, noting that economic stress can trigger emotional distress, affecting families and children's development and increasing vulnerability to social risks.
"The National Health and Morbidity Survey 2023 highlights the urgency, with nearly one million Malaysian adults facing depression and one in six children experiencing mental health issues, reflecting widespread struggles in homes, schools, workplaces and communities.
"In the digital space, risks are also rising, from financial scams and defamation to harmful online content, alongside nearly 15,000 reported cases of child sexual exploitation.
"At the same time, Malaysia is becoming an ageing society, with the elderly projected to make up eight per cent of the population by 2025, increasing demand for care, support and dignity.
"Overall, these issues show that today's social problems are deeply interconnected and cannot be addressed in isolation."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Exhaustion and sadness' in Beirut's southern suburbs
Article:
IN Beirut's southern suburbs, where Israeli strikes reduced a building complex to a metal shell, student Rana walked towards her home, though like many others she fears the full-scale eruption of war again.
The Israelis "are not reliable, they can strike again at any moment", said the young woman, refusing to give her full name.
As though to confirm her fears, Israel struck Beirut's southern suburbs again on Wednesday – the first there since massive deadly strikes across Lebanon on April 8.
A source close to Hizbollah confirmed that one of the group's commanders was killed in the strike.
"We just want to avoid being displaced again," said the 20-year-old.
Despite a ceasefire with Hizbollah that began on April 17, Israeli strikes have continued, mainly targeting southern Lebanon, though Beirut and its southern suburbs – where Hizbollah holds sway – had been spared until now.
But even so, many of the tens of thousands of displaced avoided returning to their homes in the suburbs, fearing a sudden resumption of the large-scale strikes that thrashed the area during the latest war.
Hizbollah, which has also continued to claim attacks on Israeli soldiers and territory, has not given residents the green light to return home to the suburbs, where Israeli drones continue to hover.
"It's a lot of exhaustion and sadness," Rana told AFP during a press tour organised by Hizbollah.
She described the fight between Hizbollah and Israel as "a battle between good and evil."
A Hizbollah supporter requesting anonymity meanwhile admitted that "people are still afraid."
At the start of the war, Israel called for the evacuation of Beirut's southern suburbs, which have between 600,000 and 800,000 residents.
Some have decided to return nonetheless, including Hassan Moqdad, a 37-year-old mechanic, who said with a smile: "God is the protector."
Even during the war, he had ventured out to his workshop – one of the few remaining edifices on a devastated road.
"I went back home when the ceasefire started and found light damage," he said.
Graffiti on the rubble of one of the destroyed buildings in the area read: "you destroy buildings and we destroy brigades", referring to Israeli border forces.
Nearby, a placard perched on the wreckage read: "I will make rockets from the metal debris of my destroyed home."
With his gaze fixed on a roof slumped over a pile of rubble, university lecturer Ibrahim Shukr recalled a bookshop he used to frequent, tears glistening in his eyes.
Having lived in the suburb of Haret Hreik for 35 years, he returned to his flat at the start of the truce and was happy to find it intact.
"I am sad for my neighbour and my friend's homes," which were destroyed, he said.
Standing before the rubble of his flattened building, Karim Zein, 19, said he was ready to make any sacrifice for Hizbollah.
"We do not mourn the stone, for it can be rebuilt," he said, having taken refuge with his sister in south Beirut.
He had ignored Israel's first mass evacuation warning for the southern suburbs, and only left when there was a specific warning for his building.
He nonetheless said he believed that "even in its destruction, the suburb is beautiful."
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: A-G's powers not beyond judicial scrutiny - Court of Appeal
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Attorney-General (A-G)'s prosecutorial discretion can still be reviewed by the courts in exceptional cases, the Court of Appeal ruled today.
A three-member appellate bench led by Datuk Faizah Jamaludin said the A-G's powers under Article 145(3) of the Federal Constitution were broad, but not absolute or beyond judicial scrutiny.
The judge said it can still be challenged in "appropriate, rare and exceptional circumstances" where courts may review whether the decision was lawful and rational.
Article 145(3) empowers the A-G, as public prosecutor, to institute, conduct or discontinue criminal proceedings.
Citing a previous Federal Court ruling, she said the apex court had rejected the notion that constitutional discretion was beyond scrutiny.
"It held that discretion, whether statutory or constitutional, remains an executive power and can still be reviewed by the courts, albeit under stricter scrutiny."
Faizah said this in her ruling after allowing the Malaysian Bar's judicial review application to challenge the A-G's decision to discontinue Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi's Yayasan Akalbudi corruption trial, which resulted in a discharge not amounting to an acquittal (DNAA).
Faizah said the presumption of legality cannot be used to shut out judicial review where the challenge is supported by identified and serious matters.
"In the present case, the significance of the criminal court's finding must not be understated.
"By that stage, the prosecution had closed its case and a prima facie finding had been made on all 47 charges; the defence had commenced.
"By the time the deputy public prosecutor (DPP) conveyed the A-G's decision to discontinue the prosecution and seek a DNAA, the case had moved beyond mere allegation.
"The prosecution evidence had undergone maximum evaluation by the trial judge, and the defence had already commenced," she said.
Other members of the bench were Datuk Dr Lim Hock Leng and Datuk Nadzarin Wok Nordin.
The court said it took note of the DPP's reasons for the DNAA application, including the possibility that the Attorney-General's Chambers and the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission were manipulated or exploited by certain parties.
"These are serious matters. At the leave stage, they raise a legitimate question on whether discretion was properly exercised, or whether extraneous or insufficiently grounded considerations entered the decision-making process."
The court said Zahid was charged when he did not hold public office and was appointed Deputy Prime Minister after the prosecution had established a prima facie case.
"Thereafter, six representations were made and the prosecution ultimately sought a DNAA.
"To be clear, the court makes no finding at this stage whether the decision was politically influenced; that would be premature.
"The chronology is relevant only to the question of whether the complaint is arguable at leave, not as proof of improper purpose.
"But the sequence of events, when considered together with the advanced stage of the trial and the reasons advanced by the DPP for discontinuance, is sufficient to render the Bar's complaint arguable rather than speculative," she added.
The court then ruled that the matter be remitted to the High Court for a substantive hearing on the merits.
On Sept 4, 2023, Zahid, who is also the Bagan Datuk member of parliament, was freed of all 47 graft charges he faced after the prosecution applied for a discharge not amounting to an acquittal against him.
However, Federal Court judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah, who presided as the High Court judge, refused to grant a full acquittal for Zahid, stating that the A-G had the authority to initiate and withdraw charges at any time before a judgment is rendered.
Sequerah also noted that judicial time and a large amount of taxpayers' money would have been wasted if the prosecution decided not to proceed with the case.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Coronation Square to transform Johor Baru, generate 60,000 jobs
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A major milestone in Johor Baru's city centre transformation was marked today with the groundbreaking of Coronation Square, a RM5 billion integrated development set to anchor the next phase of growth within the Ibrahim International Business District (IIBD).
Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the project reflected Johor's rising momentum as a key economic powerhouse, bolstered by strong investor confidence and deepening cross-border integration with Singapore.
The ceremony, attended by Singapore's Consul-General Ng Kuan Khai and key investors, signals growing confidence in Johor's strategic role within the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ).
Onn Hafiz said Johor stood at an "inflection point", with RM110 billion in approved investments recorded in 2025, generating more than 24,000 jobs despite global economic headwinds.
"Coronation Square is not a standalone project but a critical component of a wider, coordinated effort to reposition Johor Baru as a competitive metropolitan hub," he said.
Strategically located near the Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) complex and directly linked to the Johor Baru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link due by end-2026, the development is poised to enhance cross-border mobility and economic activity.
The project will feature what is set to be Johor Baru's largest retail mall, alongside residential towers, hospitality, healthcare and commercial components, targeting both domestic demand and a growing influx of Singapore-linked traffic.
Onn Hafiz said the development would play a pivotal role in revitalising the city centre, strengthening connectivity and delivering inclusive economic benefits.
Coronation Square is projected to generate over RM9 billion in economic spin-offs and create up to 60,000 jobs throughout its lifecycle.
Developer Coronade Properties managing director Datuk Patrick Lim described the project as a "city within a city", designed to integrate businesses, infrastructure and community living.
"This is more than a retail destination. It is an ecosystem built to support economic activity and enhance quality of life," he said.
In a parallel move, the Singapore Retailers Association (SRA) and CapitaLand Investment (CLI) signed a memorandum of understanding to facilitate the entry of Singapore brands into Johor, including pilot retail concepts and market expansion initiatives.
CLI, which manages over 70 malls across Asia, has been appointed retail advisor, while its lodging arm, The Ascott Limited, will operate the hotel component, marking its debut in Johor Baru.
The collaboration is expected to accelerate Johor Baru's emergence as a regional retail and lifestyle hub within the Malaysia-Singapore corridor.
With its integrated design and direct connectivity to major transport nodes, Coronation Square is expected to catalyse urban regeneration, intensify economic clustering and reinforce Johor's position as a gateway city in the JS-SEZ.
Onn Hafiz said the project aligned with the Maju Johor 2030 vision, which prioritises high-income growth, quality jobs and sustainable urban development.
"The importance of today's groundbreaking goes beyond construction. It reflects Johor's commitment to move forward as a modern, connected and competitive state," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: Pas must drop 'DNA of betrayal'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said Pas must remove the "DNA of betrayal" in its political relationships.
His remarks came after Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang, in his regular coloumn Minda Presiden, urged Umno to reflect and "return to the right path" after allegedly choosing the wrong political partners.
"I was the one who initiated (efforts to unite the Malay-Muslim community through) Muafakat Nasional.
"But their DNA is betrayal. So if they remove that sense of betrayal, maybe we can consider cooperation.
"But if that betrayal continues, I think it would be better to forget it," Zahid told reporters on the sidelines of the launch of the National Social Policy Action Plan 2026–2030 today.
Hadi said Umno had chosen the wrong political partners by cooperating with PKR and DAP, with the two Pakatan Harapan parties leading it to place less emphasis on policies advancing Malay and Muslim interests.
Hadi, who is the Marang MP, said Umno was willing to abandon its Islamic and Malay principles by working with liberal parties such as PKR and extremely secular parties such as DAP in exchange for government posts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No special grounds for Najib to obtain stay to pay US$1.3b, says SRC
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: SRC International Sdn Bhd today argued that there are no special circumstances warranting a stay of execution for former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak pending his appeal against a High Court ruling holding him
liable
for the company's US$1.18 billion losses arising from misfeasance in public office.
SRC lawyer P. Gananathan submitted that Najib's prominence as a public figure does not entitle him to treatment different from that accorded to any judgment debtor, nor does it amount to special circumstances justifying a stay.
"If one were to treat persons who face large monetary judgment because of their stature in government or business, or if one were to treat them with special gloves because they are a person of public prominence, then the law is not deemed equal.
"I have read the affidavits by the first defendant (Najib) and I have not come across any statement that he is unable to pay.
"If the threshold to meet special circumstances had not been met, then the court will leave it as that," said Gananathan, who appeared with Kwan Will Sen during the hearing of the stay application.
The hearing was held before High Court judge Datuk Ahmad Fairuz Zainol Abidin, who now sits at the Court of Appeal.
On March 31, Fairuz ordered the former Pekan member of parliament to pay SRC a total of US$1.3 billion, which includes US$120 million from SRC-linked funds that had been credited into Najib's personal account.
Gananathan said Najib's claim that paying US$1.3 billion to SRC would cause him significant losses and fear of reputational damage were irrelevant.
"The personal hardship of Najib are the ordinary consequences that any defendant would face in consequence of a monetary judgment.
"While the monetary judgment of US$1.18 billion granted in favour of the plaintiff (SRC) is sizable, this alone is insufficient to warrant the court's intervention on the premise that the appeal would be rendered nugatory.
"Any perceived difficulty on the part of Najib to satisfy the monetary judgment does not amount to the appeal being rendered nugatory," he said.
Gananathan also rebutted Najib's contention that SRC's winding-up on Jan 19 this year and its current liquidation made the likelihood of recovering any substantial sum, in the event his appeal succeeds, extremely low and unrealistic.
He said the winding-up order directed that all monies received by the liquidator be paid into the SRC Group Trust account.
"As such, any sums recovered in execution of the judgment herein will ultimately be held and administered by the Ministry of Finance Inc, effectively held by the government for the benefit of the plaintiff.
"The liquidator under his duties of recovery and powers vested upon him in liquidation under the Companies Act will be subject to the same restitutionary obligations.
"As officer of the court, the liquidator is answerable to the court and would be under a duty to ensure that the monies held in trust are restituted in the event of a successful appeal," he said.
Meanwhile, Najib's lawyer Muhammad Farhan Muhammad Shafee argued that special circumstances existed due to the unprecedented and astronomical scale of the judgment sum, which he said could result in irreversible and disproportionate financial consequences for his client.
He said granting a stay of execution would not prejudice SRC and was necessary to ensure the appeal process remained meaningful rather than becoming a mere academic exercise.
"If execution proceeds, Najib would be compelled to initiate fresh, costly and uncertain legal proceedings to recover billions in assets that may have already been dissipated through the SRC's winding-up process," he said.
Farhan said that given SRC has been wound up and is currently in liquidation, there is a legitimate and real risk that the substantial sum of US$1.3 billion would be impossible to recover should Najib's appeal succeed.
Fairuz set May 13 to deliver his decision on the stay application.
SRC is seeking, among others, a declaration that Najib is liable to account for and repay US$1.18 billion, said to be losses arising from the company's proposed investment funds.
The company is also seeking a declaration for Najib to return US$120 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SC's investED delivers talent boost to capital market
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Securities Commission's (SC) flagship graduate programme, investED, continues to strengthen Malaysia's capital market talent pool, with 65 per cent of participants from its leadership initiative securing jobs within the industry.
SC chairman Datuk Mohammad Faiz Azmi said a sustainable talent pipeline is necessary to support the Capital Market Masterplan 2026–2030 which is projected to increase market size to between RM5.8 trillion and RM6.3 trillion by 2030.
"investED is aligned with industry needs. It is a credible and effective capacity building platform, delivering tangible workforce outcomes for the Malaysian capital market.
"By combining structured training with on-the-job exposure, it ensures that participants are ready for their roles and capable of contributing to the industry,'' he said.
Meanwhile, partner firms and investED steering committee members comprising industry players lauded the programme's impact on talent development.
Bursa Malaysia chief executive officer and steering committee member Datuk Fad'l Mohamed said programmes such as investED provide participants with early exposure and practical insights into the industry and its ecosystem.
"Over the course of the programme, the cohort gained knowledge and skills that we hope will support their future growth and contribution to Malaysia's capital market," said Fad'l.
Since its introduction, the commission said 581 trainees have completed the investED Leadership Programme, with graduates now employed across various capital market segments.
"The remaining graduates are employed across other sectors including banking, fintech, consulting and accounting.
"This affirms the programme's objective in building a sustainable and skilled talent pipeline for the capital market," it said.
ISC said the investED Foundation programme has enrolled 3,072 participants over three cohorts, surpassing its target of 2,400.
It noted that this foundation programme provides introductory knowledge and baseline certification on the capital market.
Since its inception in 2023, investED has undertaken significant outreach, engaging more than 190,000 university students and fresh graduates nationwide through career fairs and talks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia's Sumatra flood victims file lawsuit as reconstruction work stalls
Article:
JAKARTA: Victims of last year's deadly floods in Indonesia's Sumatra filed a lawsuit to the state court on Thursday, urging the government to grant national disaster status to three affected provinces and suspend approvals for new forest use permits.
The state administrative court received the lawsuit documents on Thursday, the court's website showed.
Seven residents of Aceh, North Sumatra and West Sumatra filed the suit against Indonesia's president, its environment, forestry and agriculture ministers as well as the head of the country's disaster mitigation agency, said Diki Rafiqi, one of the petitioners.
They demanded that the government immediately grants national disaster status to the three affected provinces, with the existing reconstruction process now stalled because of the limited budget allocated by the provincial governments, Diki told Reuters.
If national disaster status is granted, the central government must use the state budget to pay for the reconstruction process, which includes the building of temporary accommodation or permanent new housing for the people affected.
"Many residents still do not have temporary houses ... This is the most basic thing and it's due to the local government's limited financial capacity," Diki said.
The petitioners also demanded the government to impose a moratorium on the issuing of new forest use, mining and plantation permits until land in the three provinces has been completely rehabilitated, Diki said.
They also asked the government to review existing mining, forest use and plantation permits, he added.
At least 1,200 people were killed and 300,000 homes damaged from the cyclone-induced floods and landslides last year.
Environmental groups said that the impact of the storms was exacerbated by rapid deforestation in Sumatra.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Countries scramble to track passengers of virus-hit cruise ship
Article:
AMSTERDAM: Countries worldwide scrambled on Thursday to trace people who had left the cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak before it got marooned off the coast of Cape Verde, in an effort to prevent further spread of the disease.
Three people - a Dutch couple and a German national - died in the outbreak on the MV Hondius. Eight people, including a Swiss citizen, are suspected to have contracted the virus, according to the World Health Organisation.
The Dutch government on Wednesday said around 40 passengers had disembarked the ship in Santa Helena, where the ship made a stop on its way to Cape Verde - before the outbreak was reported.
The whereabouts of many of these passengers is as yet unknown. One of those to disembark was the wife of the Dutchman who had died aboard the ship on April 11.
She fell sick herself and died before she could reach the Netherlands.
Dutch airline KLM on Wednesday said it had taken the woman off a plane in Johannesburg on April 25 due to her deteriorating medical condition.
The virus found in the victims has been confirmed as the Andean strain, which can spread among humans through very close contact.
Experts have stressed that contagion is very rare, but the outbreak has put health authorities on high alert.
The United States' Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) said on Wednesday it was closely monitoring the situation with US travellers on board the ship, adding that the risk to the American public was extremely low at the time.
French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot on Thursday said one French citizen had been in contact with a person who had fallen ill but was not currently showing symptoms.
Argentina's health ministry has said it will carry out rodent trapping and analysis in the southern city of Ushuaia, the origin point of the cruise ship.
The MV Hondius, with nearly 150 people on board, is expected to dock in Spain's Tenerife, in the Canary Islands, by Saturday.
Once in Tenerife, if they are still healthy, all non-Spanish citizens will be repatriated to their countries, while 14 Spanish passengers will be quarantined in a military hospital in Madrid.
Three patients were evacuated from the ship on Wednesday. One of them has been admitted to a hospital in the Netherlands, while another one was transferred to Germany for medical care.
The plane carrying the third patient was set to land in the Netherlands early on Thursday, after facing a delay due to a problem with the patient's life support system.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PNB's Abdul Rahman Ahmad named Sime Darby's new chairman
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sime Darby Bhd has appointed Permodalan Nasional Bhd (PNB) president and group chief executive officer (PGCE) Datuk Abdul Rahman Ahmad as its new chairman with immediate effect.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, Sime Darby said Abdul Rahman, 56, was appointed as a non-independent and non-executive chairman.
Abdul Rahman previously served as group chief executive officer of CIMB Group Holdings Bhd and chief executive officer of CIMB Bank Bhd from 2020 to 2024.
He will retire as PNB president and group chief executive on May 31.
Abdul Rahman was appointed president and group chief executive of PNB on July 1, 2024 as he left CIMB Group and took over from Datuk Ahmad Zulqarnain Onn who was tasked to helm the Employees Provident Fund.
He had also previously served as PNB president and group chief executive between 2016 and 2020.
"Abdul Rahman has held senior leadership positions across a broad range of sectors, including as group chief executive officer of Malaysian Resources Corp Bhd and Media Prima Bhd, and as chief executive officer of Ekuiti Nasional Bhd," the filing said.
He also served as the non-independent non-executive chairman of Sime Darby from 2019 to 2020 and Velesto Energy Bhd from 2017 to 2019.
Abdul Rahman holds a Master of Arts in Economics from Cambridge University and is a member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants in England and Wales and the Malaysian Institute of Accountants.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Media professionals should nurture market-savvy, entrepreneurial skills, says RTM DG
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Media practitioners should equip themselves with entrepreneurial skills and a strong understanding of market dynamics, as Malaysia moves towards building a more robust entrepreneurial ecosystem, said Radio Televisyen Malaysia director-general of broadcasting Ashwad Ismail.
He said that amid growing emphasis on developing a broader base of entrepreneurs in the country, including among young journalists and content creators, it is crucial to support the target of strengthening small and medium enterprises' (SMEs) participation and fostering a more innovation-driven economy.
"I think to be a great entrepreneurial nation, we need more than 1.3 million SMEs in Malaysia. We need at least 25 per cent of our society to be entrepreneurs, which would be about seven million people.
"We should focus on this, especially the young ones (young journalists and content creators) who should also learn entrepreneurship," he told reporters after a sharing session on "Sustaining Media in Times of Crisis: Challenges and Revenue Realities" at the National Journalists' Day (Hawana) 2026 Media Forum here today.
Commenting on the role of artificial intelligence (AI), Ashwad, who is also a board member of the Malaysian Media Council, said that among the key challenges are how media players can gain public trust and how mainstream media can be more effective in helping people make well-informed decisions in times of crisis.
"It is not just about social obligation, but also striking a balance and ensuring the media industry can sustain itself in the long run as a business. I think that balance is crucial.
"We understand that in the age of AI, and with much of the discussion happening around social media platforms, these are the areas we have to work on by continuing to engage not just the public and all stakeholders, but also international players," he added.
He said it is important for the media industry to come together and engage more effectively with global technology companies, as collective action would strengthen the industry's voice and better position it to adapt to rapid changes in the digital and AI-driven media landscape.
"There is no longer traffic migration solely from social media. Based on data, close to 40 per cent of people now search not only on Google but also on AI platforms such as ChatGPT and DeepSeek.
"So are we going to fight technology companies? In my personal opinion, do not fight them. Work with them, find ways to collaborate, and work across sectors," he said.
The forum, organised by the Malaysian National News Agency (Bernama), brings together heads of media organisations, including those from Sabah and Sarawak, representatives of foreign media agencies in Malaysia, and various stakeholders in the national media landscape.
For the first time, the Hawana Media Forum is being held as a one-day event, in conjunction with the upcoming Hawana 2026 celebration on June 20, to encourage more in-depth discussions and a more meaningful exchange of ideas.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: CelcomDigi, Media Prima explore digital collaboration
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: CelcomDigi, the country's largest mobile service operator, visited Media Prima Bhd to explore collaboration in advancing the digital ecosystem and communication platforms.
"We believe there is strong synergy in digital innovation, content distribution, and the enrichment of Malaysia's digital ecosystem," Media Prima, the nation's leading integrated media group, said in a statement.
CelcomDigi was led by chief executive officer Albern Murty and received by Media Prima's senior management, including group managing director Datuk Rafiq Razali and group chief financial officer Rosli Sabarudin.
Also present were CelcomDigi chief strategy, transformation and regulatory officer Datuk Kamal Khalid, chief corporate affairs officer Joachim Rajaram, and Media Prima News and Current Affairs group managing editor Jasbant Singh.
The visit began with a tour of Media Prima's broadcast and digital facilities, including Media Prima Audio, the News and Current Affairs studios, Rev Media Group, and the editorial floor.
The delegation was briefed on Media Prima's integrated operations across radio, television, and digital platforms.
During the visit to the TV3 studio, Albern also read the news as a broadcast journalist.
Upon the conclusion of the visit, Media Prima hosted a networking lunch and marked the birthday of Kamal.
Formed on Nov 30, 2022, through the merger of Celcom and Digi, CelcomDigi serves over 20 million users nationwide.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Defence minister to discuss with Norway after missile export halt hits LCS project
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — Malaysia’s Defence Ministry ‌said it will discuss with Norway a ​shift in Oslo’s position regarding defence export approvals that has halted the supply of missiles intended for its ‌naval combat ships, in the latest blow to the project.
In a ​statement on Thursday, Defence Minister Mohamed Khaled Nordin confirmed that the ministry holds a procurement contract with Norwegian defence firm Kongsberg Defence & Aerospace AS for the supply of ​Naval Strike Missile (NSM) guided missiles which are used to equip the country’s Littoral Combat Ships (LCS).
Kongsberg Defence & Aerospace AS told Reuters export licensing decisions are handled by the Norwegian authorities and that it fully complies with all applicable regulations, adding that it remains ‌committed to its long-term partnerships and activities in the region.
The Norwegian ⁠Embassy in Kuala Lumpur directed queries ⁠to its Foreign Affairs Ministry.
Malaysia’s LCS project, awarded ⁠in 2011 for six ⁠vessels at an initial ⁠cost of RM6 billion, has been dogged by serious mismanagement problems where the cost of it has ballooned over the years while ⁠the ships remained undelivered.
The project was relaunched in 2023 following a government review, with the decision made to acquire only five ships instead of six. The first vessel, which was originally scheduled for delivery in August this year, was pushed back to December due to late ⁠equipment deliveries from manufacturers and ongoing rework issues.
The statement came in response to a report on a Malaysian defence news portal ⁠that Norway had banned the deliveries of the missiles to Malaysia just days ⁠before ⁠the scheduled date.
“The Ministry takes note of the change in the Norwegian government’s ​position regarding defence export approvals, which has ​affected the implementation of the said ‌contract,” Mohamed Khaled said.
Mohamed Khaled added that ​they will seek further clarification and ​discuss appropriate steps to safeguard the nation’s interests. — Reuters

Title: Transport Ministry: JPJ to accept foreign licence conversion applications for Malaysians nationwide from June 1
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 7 — Malaysian citizens holding valid foreign driving licences with no restrictions may apply to convert them into Malaysian Driving Licences (LMM), subject to record checks and compliance with standard operating procedures (SOPs) set by the Road Transport Department (JPJ).
In a statement today, the Transport Ministry (MOT) said applications under the improved procedures can be made at all JPJ offices nationwide beginning June 1.
“The move is in line with current developments and aims to ensure the mobility of Malaysians working, residing or managing affairs abroad is not disrupted,” the ministry said.
Under the current policy, conversion of foreign driving licences to Malaysian licences is only allowed for Malaysians who obtained foreign driving licences before holding a Malaysian licence, participants of the Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) programme and applicants under the diplomatic corps category.
MOT said the improvements were also intended to ensure driver licensing matters in the country comply with existing regulations and maintain applicants’ driving competency standards.
“It is also aimed at facilitating applications for the conversion of foreign driving licences to Malaysian licences by taking into account current needs and the mobility of Malaysians, especially amid the current challenging global and domestic economic environment,” it said.
The ministry said application requirements and procedures, including the fees involved, would be available on JPJ’s official website at www.jpj.gov.my beginning June 1. — Bernama

Title: Police seize RM16.3m worth of meth, fentanyl in Tawau raids, two charged under Dangerous Drugs Act
Article:
TAWAU, May 7 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) seized 323.91 kilogrammes (kg) of methamphetamine and 0.98 litres of fentanyl, with a total estimated value of RM16.34 million, during Operation Hymenoptera conducted on April 21 and May 1 in Tawau.
Bukit Aman Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said that in the first raid at Batu 15 Jalan Apas, the police seized 128 transparent plastic packets suspected to contain methamphetamine, weighing approximately 130.60 kg, and a Toyota Harrier vehicle.
He said two local men aged 36 and 52 were also arrested in the raid, with the first suspect believed to be the ‘transporter’ and testing positive for methamphetamine and amphetamine, while the second suspect is a syndicate member.
He said that following the arrest, a second raid was conducted on two vehicles, a Nissan Sylphy and a Toyota Camry, in Bandar Sri Indah, Batu 10, and that it is believed that the syndicate has been active since the beginning of this year.
“In the Nissan vehicle, the police found methamphetamine weighing 90.41 kg and a liquid suspected to be fentanyl weighing 0.98 litres, while in the Toyota Camry, 99 transparent plastic packets suspected to contain methamphetamine weighing 102.90 kg were found,” he said at a press conference at the Tawau police headquarters here today.
Hussein Omar said the case is being investigated under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, and if convicted, the offender can be sentenced to death by hanging or life imprisonment and caning of not less than 12 strokes.
He said the suspects were charged in court yesterday (May 6), and both defendants have records of drug offences and past criminal activities, and that action was also taken under the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 (Forfeiture of Property) 1988, with a total seizure amounting to RM34,000.
He said the syndicate’s modus operandi involved using second-hand vehicles to hide drugs, in the vehicle’s interior, which were brought from Sepanggar Port before being sent to Tawau.
Hussein Omar said Tawau has become a transit hub for smuggling prohibited substances, not only for local use but also for sale or distribution to other countries such as the Philippines and Indonesia.
“The police believe that the drugs came from the Golden Triangle or the northern part of Peninsular Malaysia, passing through the Klang Valley and smuggled into Sabah and Sarawak for use here and in neighbouring countries,” he said, adding that the mastermind of the syndicate had fled the country, while syndicate members had been arrested. — Bernama

Title: High Court to decide May 13 on Najib’s bid to stay US$1.18b SRC payout
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 7 — The High Court will decide on May 13 whether Datuk Seri Najib Razak can obtain a stay of execution on the US$1.3 billion (about RM5.08 billion) judgment awarded to SRC International Sdn Bhd pending the disposal of his appeal.
Court of Appeal judge Datuk Ahmad Fairuz Zainol Abidin, sitting as the High Court trial judge, will deliver the decision, according to a report in
The Edge
.
The application concerns a March 31 judgment that ordered Najib to pay US$1.3 billion to SRC, comprising US$1.18 billion in losses suffered by the company and a separate US$120 million in company funds that entered his private bank account.
Najib’s lawyer Muhammad Farhan Shafee argued that the size of the judgment, imposed on an individual rather than a company, amounted to a special circumstance warranting a stay.
He also cited Najib’s imprisonment, his other court-related liabilities involving SRC, 1MDB and tax matters, and SRC’s winding-up in January 2026, arguing that recovery would be unrealistic if Najib’s appeal succeeds.
SRC’s lawyer P Gananathan opposed the stay, arguing that Najib had not met the legal threshold of showing special circumstances to justify pausing enforcement of the judgment.
SRC’s lawyer P. Gananathan opposed the stay, arguing that personal hardship, the size of the sum, reputational loss and concern over recovery do not amount to special circumstances.
“However sympathetic those facts may appear, there remains consequence of enforcement. They are not, in law, exceptional,” he was quoted as saying.
He said any money recovered would be placed in the SRC Group Trust Account operated by Minister of Finance Incorporated for the benefit of the SRC group.

Title: Perak govt pledges support for family of college student slain in Kelantan, says state exco
Article:
